description: List all files including hidden ones with detailed info

parts.desc: list directory contents
long format — shows permissions, owner, size, and modification date
include entries starting with . (hidden files and directories)

description: Copy a directory and all its contents to a new location

parts.desc: copy files and directories
recursive — required to copy a directory and everything inside it
the source directory to copy from
the destination path to copy to

description: Rename a file, or move it to a different path

parts.desc: move (rename) files and directories
the current filename or path
the new filename or destination path

description: Delete a directory and all its contents — no confirmation, no recovery

parts.desc: remove files or directories
recursive — removes directory contents before the directory itself
force — no prompts, ignores missing files, suppresses errors
the target directory to permanently delete

description: Recursively find all .log files starting from the current directory

parts.desc: search for files and directories in a directory tree
the starting directory for the search (current directory)
match entries whose name ends in .log (shell glob pattern)
restrict results to regular files only, not directories or symlinks

description: Make a script executable by the owner and readable/runnable by everyone

parts.desc: change file mode bits (permissions)
octal permission mask: owner=rwx (7), group=r-x (5), others=r-x (5)
the file to change permissions on

description: Change the owner and group of a file

parts.desc: change file owner and group
the new owner username and group name, separated by a colon
the file to change ownership of

description: Search all files recursively for lines containing TODO, showing line numbers

parts.desc: search file contents for lines matching a pattern
search recursively through all subdirectories
prefix each matching line with its line number
the literal string (or regular expression) to search for
the directory to search (current directory)

description: Replace every occurrence of "foo" with "bar" directly in a file

parts.desc: stream editor — applies editing commands to each line of input
edit the file in place (overwrites the original)
substitution: replace foo→bar, g means all occurrences per line
the file to modify

description: Print the 1st and 3rd comma-separated columns from a CSV file

parts.desc: pattern-directed scanning and text processing language
set the input field separator to a comma (default is whitespace)
action: print the 1st and 3rd fields of each line
the input file to process

description: Sort a CSV file numerically by the second column

parts.desc: sort lines of text
use a comma as the field separator
sort by the second field
numeric sort — compare as numbers, not strings
the file to sort

description: Count and rank unique lines in a log file, highest count first

parts.desc: print the log file contents to stdout
sort lines so duplicates become consecutive
collapse consecutive duplicate lines, prefix each with its count
sort numerically (-n) in reverse (-r) — highest count first

description: Strip comment lines and blank lines from a config file

parts.desc: -v inverts the match: print lines that do NOT start with #
the input file
filter out blank lines (^$ matches a line with nothing in it)

description: Compress a directory into a gzip-compressed tar archive

parts.desc: tape archiver — bundles files and directories into a single file
combined: -c create, -z gzip compress, -f next arg is the filename
the name of the output archive file
the directory to include in the archive

description: Extract a gzip-compressed tar archive into a specified directory

parts.desc: tape archiver
combined: -x extract, -z gzip decompress, -f next arg is the filename
the archive file to read from
change to this directory before extracting
the destination directory for the extracted files

description: Find all running processes matching a name

parts.desc: report a snapshot of current running processes
a=all users, u=user-oriented format (owner, CPU, memory), x=include daemons
filter the process list to lines containing 'nginx'

description: Immediately force-terminate a process by its PID

parts.desc: send a signal to a process
send SIGKILL — cannot be caught, blocked, or ignored by the process
the process ID (PID) of the process to terminate

description: List processes that have a socket open on port 8080

parts.desc: list open files — in Unix, network sockets are file descriptors
select files related to internet network connections
filter to connections on port 8080

description: Send a JSON POST request to an API endpoint

parts.desc: transfer data to or from a server using various protocols
use the HTTP POST method instead of the default GET
add a request header specifying the body format as JSON
the request body data to send
the URL of the API endpoint

description: Log into a remote server with SSH using a private key file

parts.desc: OpenSSH client — opens an encrypted shell session to a remote host
use this private key file for public-key authentication
the account (user) and server (host / IP address) to connect to

description: Sync a local directory to a remote server, showing transfer progress

parts.desc: fast, incremental file transfer — only sends changed data
archive mode: preserves permissions, timestamps, symlinks, and owner
verbose — print the name of each file as it is transferred
compress file data during the transfer to save bandwidth
show per-file transfer progress and speed
local source directory (trailing slash syncs contents, not the folder itself)
the remote destination in user@host:/path format

description: Show disk space usage of all mounted filesystems in human-readable units

parts.desc: report filesystem disk space usage
human-readable sizes — prints K, M, G instead of raw 512-byte blocks

description: Show the total disk usage of each item in the current directory

parts.desc: estimate file space usage
summarize — one total per argument instead of listing each subdirectory
human-readable sizes (K, M, G)
end of options — ensures filenames starting with - aren't treated as flags
shell glob expanding to every file and directory in the current directory

description: Show a compact ASCII branch graph of the full commit history

parts.desc: show the commit history
condense each commit to one line: short hash + subject
draw an ASCII art graph of branch and merge structure
include commits reachable from all branches and tags

description: Restore the most recently stashed changes and remove the stash entry

parts.desc: commands for temporarily shelving uncommitted changes
apply the most recent stash entry and drop it from the stash list

description: Show what changes are staged and ready for the next commit

parts.desc: show differences between two states
compare the index (staging area) to HEAD — shows what will be committed

description: Interactively rewrite the last 3 commits — reorder, squash, edit, or drop them

parts.desc: reapply a sequence of commits on top of a different base
interactive mode — opens an editor to pick, squash, edit, or drop commits
start from 3 commits before the current HEAD

description: Undo the last commit but keep all changed files staged, ready to re-commit

parts.desc: move HEAD (and optionally the index and working tree) to a different commit
only move HEAD — leave the index unchanged so files stay staged
the target commit: one step before the current HEAD
